summary: Moved the nested union `ColorOverlay_x8_t` out of `struct ColorOverlay` and made it a standalone union in `src/melee/lb/types.h`, preserving the pointer field type and size assertions. The PR title says this fixed an m2c crash, suggesting the nested type declaration was problematic for tooling even though the C layout stayed equivalent.
what_changed: Extracted `union ColorOverlay_x8_t` from an inline/nested declaration inside `struct ColorOverlay` to file scope.
Changed `ColorOverlay::x8_ptr1` from an inline-defined `union ColorOverlay_x8_t { ... }*` field to `union ColorOverlay_x8_t* x8_ptr1`.
Kept the union members unchanged: `GXColor light_color`, `light_rot1`, `light_rot2`, and `unk` bitfield views.
Moved `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(union ColorOverlay_x8_t) == 0x4)` to immediately follow the standalone union definition.
Preserved `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct ColorOverlay) == 0x80)`, indicating the struct layout was intended to remain unchanged.
smart_moves: Fixed a tool-facing issue by refactoring only the type declaration shape, not the binary layout.
Kept explicit `STATIC_ASSERT` checks for both the standalone union size and containing struct size, guarding against accidental ABI/layout drift.
Promoted the anonymous/nested-style type pattern into a reusable file-scope type, which is friendlier to decompilation tools that need to parse or emit references to named types.
Made `x8_ptr1` simpler and clearer as a pointer to a previously declared union type.
decomp_lessons: If m2c or related tooling crashes on a header type, check for nested named unions/structs inside containing structs; moving the type to file scope may preserve layout while avoiding parser/tool limitations.
When refactoring declarations to satisfy tooling, pair the change with `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...))` so matching work can trust that object sizes did not change.
For fields that are pointers to complex packed or bitfield views, a standalone named union can improve reuse and reduce tool fragility without changing generated code.
Header cleanup can be a legitimate matching/tooling fix even when no source logic changes.
naming_conventions: `ColorOverlay_x8_t` names the helper union after its containing concept and field offset, matching the existing decomp convention for unknown or partially understood layout types.
Unknown fields continue to use offset-based names such as `x8_ptr1`, `xC_loop`, `x10_ptr2`, `x18_alloc`, and bitfield placeholders like `x0_0` through `x0_7`.
Semantic names are retained where known, such as `light_color`, `light_rot1`, `light_rot2`, `light_enable`, and `timer`.
The PR preserves the `union TypeName` spelling rather than introducing a typedef alias.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This was a declaration-only change in a header; no evidence in the slice indicates assembly was altered.
The tactic was layout-preserving type extraction: move a nested union to file scope while keeping field offsets, bit widths, signedness, and size assertions intact.
Bitfield widths in `ColorOverlay_x8_t` remain explicit, including 6/13/13, 1-bit flag fields plus 12/12 signed fields, and 6/26 timer packing.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only stated motivation is the PR title, `Fix m2c crash`; the exact crash mode or reviewer rationale is not described in the available evidence.
follow_up_queries: Search for other nested named structs or unions in headers that may cause m2c parser crashes.
Check whether m2c has known limitations around nested named union declarations used as pointer fields.
Look for other `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(union ...))` declarations placed after containing structs that could be moved closer to standalone type definitions.
Audit `ColorOverlay_x8_t` uses to see whether the standalone union enables reuse or cleaner type propagation in future decomp work.
